items.title: Iran threatens retaliation against US over Kharg Island attack
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US President Trump urged other nations to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz, after Iran threatened escalation across the Middle East to retaliate for Washington's bombing of its main Gulf island energy hub.

#Iran #Israel #War #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Oscars 2026: A race wide open
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Hollywood will gather for the annual Academy Awards on March 15, and for most of the top prizes — it's anyone's race.
 
"One Battle After Another," starring Leonardo DiCaprio, was seen as the early favorite for the coveted best picture prize after stacking up trophy after trophy at recent ceremonies.
 
But vampire story "Sinners," a celebration of blues music and Black culture in the Segregation-era U.S. South, made a late surge.
 
Here's what you need to know about the frontrunners.
 
#oscars #sinners #onebattleafteranother #movie #hollywood

👉  Subscribe: https://reut.rs/4b8fRGn

items.title: Iran threatens to retaliate against region after US attack on Kharg Island | Reuters World News
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Iran warns it may target U.S. “hideouts” in the United Arab Emirates, as a drone attack disrupts oil-loading at the UAE’s Fujairah oil hub. European leaders push back as Washington issues a sanctions waiver on Russian crude. Global conflicts increase the risks for airline pilots and airports. Kenya reports its latest arrest in a widening ant‑smuggling crackdown. Plus, we look at the standout trends expected on the Oscars red carpet.

#news  #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Golden Globe and Grammy winner Don Cheadle is paired with Emmy winner Ayo Edebiri, the breakout star from "The Bear," to bring the widely acclaimed family play turned movie, "Proof," back to Broadway.

items.title: Temple Israel director describes chaotic escape after attack
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Temple Israel's Executive Director Jason Plotkin was in his office on a Zoom call on Thursday (March 12) when he heard what sounded like 'many bookshelves falling.' Chaos followed soon after, and Plotkin said his 'instincts took over' as he managed to escape.

items.title: Three killed in Russian strike near Kyiv, police say
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>'There's no way Russia will stop,' said a resident of a damaged building in Brovary, Ukraine, as a Russian overnight strike killed three people and injured at least eight.

items.title: Essence Black Women in Hollywood
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Black women from the film and television industry came together for the 19th annual Black Women in Hollywood luncheon ahead of the Oscars.
 
This year, the U.S. magazine honored actor Kerry Washington, "Sinners" producer Zinzi Coogler, and newcomer Chase Infiniti.
 
Actor and director LaTanya Richardson Jackson's legacy in the industry was also celebrated.
 
#oscars #academyawards #chaseinfinity #actors #film

items.title: Oscar-nominated film honors frontline journalists, producer says
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Following the work of photojournalist Brent Renaud until he was killed in the war in Ukraine, Oscar-nominated "Armed Only with a Camera: The Life and Death of Brent Renaud" seeks to pay tribute to front-line storytellers, Colombian producer Juan Arredondo said.

#Oscars #journalism #Ukraine #award</article>

items.title: Mexico zoo welcomes African leopard cubs
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Two African leopard cubs, a male and a female, were born February 12 at Irapuato Zoo in Mexico, as part of a reproduction program for endangered species, the zoo announced.

items.title: Bombs and blackouts: Voices from the Iran-Turkey border crossing
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>At the Kapikoy border gate in eastern Turkey, travelers from Iran describe bombs, blackouts and difficult choices as hundreds cross in both directions amid the widening U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.
 
#iran #turkey #iranwar #refugee #israel</article>

items.title: Mexican security chief's merch in demand after El Mencho raid
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Mexico’s Security Minister Omar García Harfuch has gone viral, with blankets, toys and cardboard cutouts featuring his face appearing after a military raid that killed cartel leader Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes.
